name: Andy Kaufman
blurb: He drew wide attention in 1975 performing on the first season of Saturday Night Live, and his Foreign Man character became the basis for Latka Gravas on the sitcom Taxi.
bio: Andy Kaufman was an American entertainer and performance artist sometimes called an anti-comedian for his disdain of conventional jokes. He drew wide attention in 1975 performing on the first season of Saturday Night Live, and his Foreign Man character became the basis for Latka Gravas on the sitcom Taxi. Known for elaborate hoaxes, the lounge-singer alter ego Tony Clifton, and an inter-gender wrestling act, he died of lung cancer in 1984 at age 35.

timeline.ev: Performs Mighty Mouse and Foreign Man on the first season of Saturday Night Live
Films his TV special Andys Funhouse
Begins playing Latka Gravas on Taxi
Stages his Carnegie Hall show, busing the audience out for milk and cookies
Staged wrestling feud with Jerry Lawler erupts on Late Night with David Letterman

accolades: Performed on the first-ever Saturday Night Live (1975)
Two Golden Globe nominations for Taxi (1979, 1981)
Subject of the biopic Man on the Moon (1999)
